title: PayPal's Upcoming Q3 May Finally Catalyze A Turnaround
text: PayPal's upcoming Q3 earnings report may finally catalyze a turnaround. Analysts expected Q3 results to show continued momentum in core payment processing metrics. PayPal reported adjusted earnings per share of $1.20 versus $1.07 expected, and revenue of $7.85 billion versus $7.89 billion expected. The company reported better-than-expected third-quarter earnings but missed expectations on revenue. Third-quarter revenues increased to $7.85 billion from $7.42 billion in 2023. Total payment volume increased 9% year-over-year to $422.6 billion. CEO Alex Chriss focused on prioritizing profitable growth and better monetizing acquisitions like Braintree and Venmo. PayPal shares fell 7% after softer Q4 guidance calling for low single-digit growth versus analyst expectations of 5.4% to $8.46 billion.
